mnemonics: P.E.T.S. C.A.R.E. for Sparse: Polynomials Efficiently Tracked, Stored as Coefficient Arrays, Represented Efficiently
keyPoints: Polynomials are represented as arrays where the index corresponds to the exponent and the value is the coefficient
Sparse polynomials (with many zero coefficients) can be efficiently stored using a list of (coefficient, exponent) pairs
Polynomial arithmetic (addition, subtraction, multiplication) can be implemented by manipulating these data structures
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle sparse polynomials efficiently, leading to wasted memory for high-degree polynomials with few terms
Not properly aligning exponents when adding or subtracting polynomials represented as arrays
Incorrectly handling the multiplication of terms, especially when exponents are added during the process
